original_abstract: Bicycloalkanes, cubanes, and their structural derivatives have gained significant prominence as sp3-rich bioisosteres of (hetero)arenes, offering a robust strategy to improve the metabolic stability and solubility of drug candidates. To satisfy the growing need for these complex 3D scaffolds, the chemical community has pioneered a wide range of innovative synthetic strategies. This review surveys the recent advancements in our ability to construct and functionalize these high-value molecules using light-driven processes. We examine diverse structural targets and focus on reaction manifolds that provide access to: (i) [1.1.1]-bicyclopentanes (BCPs); (ii) [2.2.1]-bicyclohexanes (BCHs); (iii) [3.1.1]-bicycloheptanes (BCHeps); and (iv) cubanes, alongside other structurally related motifs. Finally, we discuss emerging trends and future perspectives on identifying new reaction paradigms to unlock further functionalized bioisosteric units for medicinal and materials science.
humanized_abstract: Over the past decade, bicycloalkanes, including derivatives of cubane, have emerged as a new class of sp3-rich bioisosteres for (hetero)arenes to address the important challenge of enhancing the metabolism stability and solubility of lead compounds. Consequently, the synthesis of these complex 3D frameworks has been achieved through a multitude of different new methodologies. In this review we highlight recent advancements in photoredox mediated syntheses of bioisosteres that have been exploited for the preparation of bicycloalkanes (BCs) derivatives as well as a range of their structural analogues including; [1.1.1]-bicyclopentanes (BCPs), [2.2.1]-bicyclohexanes (BCHs), [3.1.1]-bicycloheptanes (BCHeps) and cubanes. Finally, we look to the future in relation to the preparation of other bioisoster units derived from complex 3D frameworks that are currently in high demand within both the pharmaceutical and materials science industries.
